terms.definition: I(X; Y) = H(X) - H(X | Y); the average reduction in uncertainty about X from knowing Y (symmetric in X and Y), measured in bits when log base is 2.
H(X | Y) = -sum p(x,y) log p(x|y); average remaining uncertainty in X after Y is observed. I(X; Y) = H(X) - H(X | Y).
H(X, Y) = -sum p(x,y) log p(x,y); total uncertainty in the pair. I(X; Y) = H(X) + H(Y) - H(X, Y).
PMI(x; y) = log(p(x,y)/(p(x)p(y))); per-outcome log-likelihood ratio vs independence. I(X; Y) is the expectation of PMI under p(x,y).
NMI = I(X; Y) / sqrt(H(X) H(Y)) or similar normalization; scales MI to [0, 1] for comparing clusterings or partitions of different sizes.
In feature selection and decision trees, information gain for a split often equals I(feature; label) or the entropy reduction H(Y) - H(Y | feature)—the same MI idea applied to supervised targets.
